summary:
The correct answer is B because thyroglossal duct cysts are lined by either stratified squamous or respiratory-type epithelial cells rather than pseudostratified columnar ones.
Thyroid tissue does not line these cysts; they develop from remnants of the embryonic thyroglossus tract located along the midline of the neck between the foramen cecum and hyoid bone. Summary:

A thyroglossal duct cyst is incorrectly stated to be lined by pseudostratified columnar epithelium. It actually contains stratified squamous or respiratory-type epithelium derived from the thyroglossal duct remnant.

Thyroid tissue does not line these cysts; they develop from remnants of the embryonic thyroglossus tract located along the midline of the neck between the foramen cecum and hyoid bone. Summary:

A thyroglossal duct cyst is incorrectly stated to be lined by pseudostratified columnar epithelium. It actually contains stratified squamous or respiratory-type epithelium derived from the thyroglossal duct remnant.. Answer choice: B
